 {"id":"bd-39u","title":"Unit tests: JS runtime + shims + event loop ordering","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires correct promise/microtask ordering and shim behavior.
- This bead is the **ordering/invariants test suite** for bd-123 + bd-1f5.

Steps:
- Unit/property tests for the PiJS event loop ordering:
  - macrotask vs microtask fixpoint draining
  - timer tie-breaking stability
  - hostcall completion enqueue order stability
- Tests for cancellation + timeout behavior across the promise bridge.
- Trace-log assertions for ordering and promise resolution sequence (diffable).

Acceptance:
- Ordering invariants from bd-123 hold and are reproducible under deterministic harness control.
- These tests protect the promise that the pinned sample set can be run with stable traces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-39vap","title":"[SEC-WS7] Rollout, Operations, and Security Documentation","description":"## Purpose
Ship hardening safely with staged rollout, operational guardrails, and complete docs.

## Why This Stream Exists
Even strong controls can cause outages or user lockouts if rolled out abruptly. Operational discipline is required to preserve trust.

## Deliverables
- Shadow mode and telemetry-first rollout
- Graduated enforcement activation plan
- Operator handbook + rollback playbooks

## Exit Criteria
- [ ] Security controls can be enabled incrementally with fast recovery paths.
- [ ] On-call responders have clear runbooks for incidents and regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] All child beads are complete with linked unit-test evidence, e2e scenario evidence, and structured logging artifacts
[ ] `br dep cycles --json` returns zero cycles for this subtree

 {"id":"bd-39za","title":"PiWasm: Tests (unit + conformance + security)","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive coverage for the PiWasm bridge.

# Scope
- Unit tests: compile/instantiate success, invalid wasm, memory grow limits, trap mapping.
- Security tests: denied imports, exceeded budgets, malicious loops (interrupt handler).
- Conformance fixtures: deterministic traces for wasm-in-js scenarios.

# Acceptance
- Tests run in CI and are deterministic.
- Failure diffs include actionable context (imports shape, limits, trap reason).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:12:31.029810247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:03:38.847228042Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:03:38.493155531Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39za","depends_on_id":"bd-10s6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39za","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39za","depends_on_id":"bd-ltk7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2200,"issue_id":"bd-39za","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deferred: PiWasm bridge deferred (bd-1ry closed), no WASM-using extensions in corpus.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:03:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3am2","title":"Workstream: Error UX (actionable suggestions + context-aware hints)","description":"# Goal
Improve error UX so failures are not just "what happened" but also "what to do next" (actionable suggestions) and "why this happened here" (context-aware hints).

# Background
Pi runs in many environments (different terminals, missing system deps like `fd`, missing API keys, VCR playback mode, etc.). The port already has structured error types, but the user experience depends on:
- concise, readable rendering
- correct categorization
- a small set of high-signal remediation hints

# Principles
- Hints must be **actionable** (specific command/path/setting), not generic.
- Hints must be **non-spammy**: show at most a couple of suggestions per error.
- Hints must be **deterministic** and testable (unit/integration tests assert on messages).

# Candidate Hint Categories (examples)
- Missing API key → point to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / etc and settings override.
- Missing system dependency (`fd`, `rg`) → exact install commands per OS.
- VCR cassette missing in CI → explain `VCR_MODE` + cassette dir + how to record.
- Permission denied reading/writing files → show the path and suggest chmod/ownership checks.
- Invalid config JSON → show file path + first parse error location.
- Provider auth failure (401) → suggest `/login` or `--api-key` usage.

# Deliverables
- A mapping from core error variants → short "next steps" hints.
- Rendering integration for:
  - interactive TUI error panels
  - print mode stderr
  - RPC mode error payloads (where appropriate)
- Tests validating the most important hint paths.

# Non-Goals
- Not building a full diagnostics engine.
- Not adding web browsing or auto-fixing.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:50.853156366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:30:10.750740972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:33:19.417964249Z","close_reason":"Workstream complete: hint taxonomy/mapping (bd-1iz5), rendering integration (bd-2kj0), and common-mode tests (bd-1ywr) all closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3am2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:55Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -20,6 +20,14 @@ The configuration is a JSON object mapping **action IDs** (camelCase) to **key s
 }
 ```
 
+Use an empty array to remove a default binding entirely:
+
+```json
+{
+  "cursorUp": []
+}
+```
+
 ### Key Syntax
 
 Keys are specified as `modifier+key`.
